title: Apptainer cannot find reference from local Docker daemon
body:
### **Version of Apptainer**

```
$ apptainer --version
apptainer version 1.2.5
$ docker --version
Docker version 24.0.7, build afdd53b
```

### **Expected Behavior**
Expected build to work.

### **Actual behavior**
Apptainer seems to be incompatible with Docker daemon. Apptainer version 1.3.3 also results in the same issue.

### **Steps to reproduce this behavior**

```
$ docker pull ubuntu:22.04

$ apptainer build out.sif docker-daemon:ubuntu:22.04
INFO:    Starting build...
FATAL:   While performing build: conveyor failed to get: while converting reference: loading image from docker engine: Error response from daemon: reference does not exist.
```
but
```
$ apptainer build out.sif docker:ubuntu:22.04
```
works fine.

### **What OS/distro are you running**

```
$ cat /etc/os-release
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="20.04.6 LTS (Focal Fossa)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS"
VERSION_ID="20.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=focal
UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal
```

### **How did you install Apptainer**
Downloaded apptainer release from GitHub and then compiled and installed from source.

comments.body: Inadvertently posted on the wrong github repo
